summary: Root cause: repo context is not actually empty; it only looks sparse because the collector currently stores git status lines only. If you want richer context, update internal/repoctx/repoctx.go to include a short git ...
diff:
diff --git a/internal/repoctx/repoctx.go b/internal/repoctx/repoctx.go
--- a/internal/repoctx/repoctx.go
+++ b/internal/repoctx/repoctx.go
@@
- inspect the current implementation
+ apply the change described by the patch bundle

diff --git a/internal/repoctx/repoctx_test.go b/internal/repoctx/repoctx_test.go
--- a/internal/repoctx/repoctx_test.go
+++ b/internal/repoctx/repoctx_test.go
@@
- inspect the current implementation
+ apply the change described by the patch bundle

risks.title: Behavior Drift
risks.detail: The follow-up may change behavior beyond the user's requested fix if the source answer mixed diagnosis and implementation.

risks.title: Local Context Risk
risks.detail: Review the existing logic in internal/repoctx/repoctx.go before applying broad changes.

test_cases: Add or update one regression test that covers the primary failure path described in the source material.
Verify the happy path still passes after the change.
Add a test that cancels context early and verifies clean exit.
Add a regression test in internal/repoctx/repoctx_test.go that covers the primary failure path.
edge_cases: Empty or nil inputs that were mentioned or implied by the original issue.
Boundary cases around existing conditionals, parsing, or branching logic.
verification_steps: Run the smallest targeted test command first.
Re-run the original failing command if one was mentioned.
Review logs or output formatting for regressions after the patch.

open_questions: Which exact file and symbol should receive the first edit?
Does the repository already contain a closer existing helper or test for this behavior?
